title: Implement Priority Queue (Max Heap)
prompt:
Implement a priority queue using a binary heap stored in an array.

API:
- constructor(comparator?) -> default should create a max heap.
- size() -> number of elements.
- isEmpty() -> boolean.
- peek() -> top element (max for default).
- push(value) -> insert value.
- pop() -> remove and return top element.

You may assume all operations are valid (no pop/peek on empty).
constraintQuestions: Should the default be a max heap? (Yes.)
Do we need to support custom comparators? (Yes, optional parameter.)
Is pop required to return the top value? (Yes.)
Should size and isEmpty be O(1)? (Yes.)
Can we use an array for the heap storage? (Yes.)

walkthrough.title: Array-backed heap
walkthrough.body:
Use a binary heap stored in an array.
- parent(i) = floor((i - 1) / 2)
- left(i) = 2*i + 1
- right(i) = 2*i + 2
On push, insert at end and sift up.
On pop, swap root with last, remove last, then sift down.

codeSnippets.title: JavaScript
C#
Python

walkthrough.title: Complexity
walkthrough.body:
push: O(log n), pop: O(log n), peek/size/isEmpty: O(1).
Space: O(n).
